The absolute, massive explosion of the Hansie Cronje match-fixing scandal completely consumed the Indian sporting and legal establishment. In Andhra Pradesh, the state government fiercely battled an absolute, massive ecological catastrophe. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On May 3, 1979, the British political landscape experienced a massive, absolutely seismic shift. Margaret Thatcher, leading the Conservative Party, aggressively secured a massive, historic electoral victory, officially becoming the absolute, very first female Prime Minister of the United Kingdom. Her incredibly massive, fiercely uncompromising tenure heavily dismantled the British welfare state, aggressively crushed the powerful labor unions, and fundamentally reshaped global economics through her massive, deeply polarizing neoliberal policies, forever earning her the moniker "The Iron Lady."

On May 3, 1968, massive, historically defining civil unrest aggressively erupted in Paris, heavily sparking the legendary "May 68" protests. Initially led by incredibly militant university students heavily protesting capitalism, consumerism, and traditional institutions, the massive movement rapidly escalated. Over 10 million French workers aggressively joined the students in wildcat strikes, absolutely paralyzing the entire national economy and heavily pushing the massive government of President Charles de Gaulle to the absolute brink of total, catastrophic collapse.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On May 3, 1913, the absolute, monumental genesis of the massive Indian cinematic industry officially occurred. *Raja Harishchandra*, an incredibly massive, full-length mythological silent film brilliantly produced and aggressively directed by Dadasaheb Phalke, was commercially released at the Coronation Cinema in Bombay (Mumbai). The massive, unprecedented success of this completely indigenous film heavily proved the absolute, massive commercial viability of cinema in India, fundamentally laying the incredibly deep foundation for Bollywood.

On May 3, 1969, Dr. Zakir Husain, the massive, highly distinguished, and deeply revered third President of India, tragically passed away while in office. A brilliant, massive intellectual and a fierce advocate for modern, secular education, he heavily co-founded the massive Jamia Millia Islamia university. His sudden, massive death heavily triggered a highly sensitive constitutional transition, aggressively necessitating a new presidential election during an incredibly volatile, massive period of political factionalism within the ruling Indian National Congress.
